tr 

   1# automatically generated, DON'T EDIT. please edit init.ct from where this file stems.
   2from dbcq import dbcq
   3import cnf
   4from dip import dig
   5from tram import Sample, Idable, Amount, Identifier
   6from tram import Patient
   7from tram import Finding
   8from tram import Rec, BooleanRec, NumberRec, StringRec, DateRec, MultiRec, CatalogRec
   9from tram import User
  10import re
  11import csv
  12import sys
  13address = "address"
  14appointment = "appointment"
  15catalog = "catalog"
  16category = "category" # MASTER, ALIQUOTGROUP, DERIVED. dtype in db.
  17concentration = "concentration"
  18cxxkitid = "cxxkitid"
  19creationdate = "creationdate" 
  20derivaldate = "derivaldate" # aufteilungsdatum / date of distribution
  21email = "email"
  22initialamount = "initialamount"
  23initialunit = "initialunit"
  24first_repositiondate = "first_repositiondate" # datum der ersten einlagerung / date of first storage (not in fhir). is identical to derivaldate. first_repositiondate in db.
  25method = "method"
  26kitid = "kitid"
  27lastlogin = "lastlogin"
  28login = "login"
  29locationname = "locationname"
  30locationpath = "locationpath"
  31orga = "orga"
  32parentid = "parentid"
  33parentoid = "parentoid"
  34patientid = "patientid"
  35project = "project"
  36receiptdate = "receiptdate" # eingangsdatum / date of receipt. receiptdate in db.
  37receptacle = "receptacle"
  38repositiondate = "repositiondate" # datum der letzten einlagerung / most recent storage date. 
  39restamount = "restamount"
  40restunit = "restunit"
  41sampleid = "sampleid"
  42sampleoid = "sampleoid"
  43samplingdate = "samplingdate" # entnahmedatum / extraction date.
  44secondprocessing = "secondprocessing"
  45secondprocessingdate = "secondprocessingdate"
  46stockprocessing = "stockprocessing"
  47stockprocessingdate = "stockprocessingdate"
  48trial = "trial"
  49type = "type" # sampletype (EDTA, stool etc) in db.
  50username = "username"
  51values = "values"
  52xposition = "xposition"
  53yposition = "yposition"
  54cnftemplate = """
  55# settings for traction.
  56
  57# sampleid sets the idcontainertype code that is used when searching for sampleid.
  58# put in a code per db target.
  59sampleid: 
  60  <db target>: <an idcontainertype code, e.g. SAMPLEID>
  61# patientid sets the idcontainertype code that is used when searching for patientid.
  62# put in a code per db target.
  63patientid: 
  64  <db target>: <an idcontainertype code, e.g. LIMSPSN>
  65
  66# idc holds additional idcontainertype codes that will be queryable as command line flags.
  67idc:
  68 - <an idcontainertype code>
  69 - <another idcontainertype code>
  70
  71# cxx holds the centraxx version for db target
  72cxx:
  73  <db target>: 3|4
  74"""
  75
  76def _checkverbose(verbose, possible):
  77    """
  78     _checkverbose makes shure that only allowed keys are in the verbose array. 
  79    """
  80    for verb in verbose:
  81        if verb not in possible: 
  82            print(f"error: verbose entry {verb} must be in {possible}.")
  83            return False
  84    return True
  85def floatornull(x):
  86    """
  87     floatornull casts to float or returns None. somehow the values passed
  88     to Amount need float casts, and float casts don't accept None.
  89    """
  90    if x is None:
  91        return None
  92    return float(x)
  93def get_ids(idables:list, code:str|None=None) -> list:
  94    """
  95     get_ids returns a list of string ids from a list of Idables (Sample or
  96     Patient).
  97    """
  98    return [ x.id(code) for x in idables ]
  99def isnumber(a) -> bool:
 100    """
 101     isnumber returns if a string is a number to prevent sql injection.  is
 102     this handled better by an existing library function?
 103    """
 104    return re.match(r"^[0-9](.[0-9]*)?$", a) is not None
 105def isidentifier(a) -> bool:
 106    """
 107     isidentifier returns whether a string is a sql identifier to prevent
 108     sql injection.  like isnumber, could this be handled better by a library?
 109    """
 110    return re.match(r"^[A-Za-z_0-9\.#]+$", a) is not None
 111def _dextend(d:dict, key, lst:list|None):
 112    """
 113     _dextends extends an array in a dictionary at the given key with the
 114     values in the passed list.
 115    """
 116    if lst is not None:
 117        if key not in d:
 118            d[key] = []
 119        d[key].extend(lst)
 120def _tablelists(all:dict, cutoff:int): # -> (dict, dict)
 121    """
 122     _tablelists returns a dict of the lists smaller than cutoff and a
 123     second dict of the lists larger than cutoff that go into temporary
 124     tables.
 125    """
 126    outsmall = {}
 127    outlarge = {}
 128    for key, lst in all.items():
 129        if lst is not None and len(lst) > cutoff:
 130            outlarge[key] = lst
 131        else:
 132            outsmall[key] = lst
 133    return outsmall, outlarge
 134def _uniq(lst): # -> list
 135    """
 136     _uniq returns a new list containing the unique items of the list passed, preserving order.
 137    """
 138    out = []
 139    for e in lst:
 140        if e not in out:
 141            out.append(e)
 142    return out
 143def _byifone(alllists):
 144    """
 145     _byifone sets by to the only passed query parameter, if missing is
 146     true and there is only one query parameter.
 147    """
 148    throwtogether = list(alllists["idc"].keys()) + list(alllists["nonidc"].keys())
 149    if len(throwtogether) == 1:
 150        return throwtogether[0]
 151    else:
 152        raise Exception("--missing needs a tr constant or idc passed to --by")
 153def _prepby(alllists:dict, by:str=None, missing:bool=None):
 154    """
 155     _prepby returns a bydict that holds the query params for the by as key
 156     (if there are any), and a second list, missinglst, that holds these
 157     query params so that they can subsequently be deleted so that the
 158     truly missing remain.
 159    """
 160    bydict = None
 161    missinglst = None
 162    if by is not None:
 163        if missing:
 164            missinglst = []
 165            # todo wrap this in function: missinglst =  idc_or_not(alllists, by)
 166            if by in alllists["idc"]:
 167                missinglst = alllists["idc"][by]
 168            elif by in alllists["nonidc"]:
 169                missinglst = alllists["nonidc"][by]
 170        else:
 171            bydict = {}
 172            if by in alllists["idc"]:
 173                for byval in alllists["idc"][by]:
 174                    bydict[byval] = []
 175            if by in alllists["nonidc"]:
 176                for byval in alllists["nonidc"][by]:
 177                    bydict[byval] = []
 178    return bydict, missinglst
 179def _fillby(bydict, by, row, what):
 180    """
 181     _fillby puts a value into the bydict at the key of the by-field.
 182    """
 183    if bydict is not None:
 184        bykey = dig(row, by.lower())
 185        if bykey is not None and not bykey in bydict:
 186            bydict[bykey] = []
 187        bydict[bykey].append(what)
 188def _updatemissing(missinglst, by, row):
 189    """
 190     _updatemissing removes the value at the by-field from the missing list.
 191    """
 192    bykey = dig(row, by.lower())
 193    if bykey in missinglst:
 194        missinglst.remove(bykey)
 195
 196def idable_csv(idables:list, outfile=None, delim:str=",", *idcs) -> str|None:
 197    """
 198     idable_csv writes a list of Idables to the given csv file. the given
 199     idcontainers are included as columns. if no idcontainers are given,
 200     all are included. if True is passed as file, the output is
 201     printed. (todo pass sys.stdout instead?)
 202    """
 203    if delim is None:
 204        delim = ","
 205    if idables is None or len(idables) == 0: # todo throw error?
 206        #print("no idables")
 207        return None
 208    out = sys.stdout 
 209    if isinstance(outfile, str):
 210        out = open(outfile, "w", newline="")
 211    colnames = []
 212    for idable in idables:
 213        for col in list(idable.iddict(*idcs).keys()):
 214            if col not in colnames:
 215                colnames.append(col)
 216    #print("colnames:")
 217    #print(colnames)
 218    writer = csv.DictWriter(out, fieldnames=colnames, delimiter=delim) 
 219    writer.writeheader()
 220    for idable in idables:
 221        d = idable.iddict(*idcs)
 222        writer.writerow(d)
 223    out.close()
 224    return outfile
 225def finding_csv(findings:list, outfile=None, delim:str=",", delim_cmp:str=",") -> str:
 226    """
 227     finding_csv writes a list of Findings along their recorded values to
 228     the given csv file, the recorded values in the same row as their
 229     respective finding. if True is passed as file the output is printed. todo use sys.stdout instead of True.
 230    """
 231    if delim is None:
 232        delim = ","
 233    if delim_cmp is None:
 234        delim_cmp = ","
 235    if findings is None or len(findings) == 0: # todo throw error?
 236        print("no findings")
 237        return None
 238    fdicts = []
 239    colnames = []
 240
 241    for finding in findings:
 242        fdict = finding.__dict__.copy()
 243        del fdict["recs"]
 244        if len(colnames) == 0:
 245            colnames.extend(list(fdict.keys()))
 246        for code, rec in finding.recs.items():
 247            tkey = f"cmp_t_{rec.labval}"
 248            vkey = f"cmp_v_{rec.labval}"
 249            if tkey not in colnames:
 250                colnames.append(tkey)
 251            if vkey not in colnames:
 252                colnames.append(vkey)
 253            if isinstance(rec, BooleanRec):
 254                fdict[tkey] = "BOOL"
 255                fdict[vkey] = rec.rec
 256            if isinstance(rec, NumberRec):
 257                fdict[tkey] = "NUMBER"
 258                fdict[vkey] = rec.rec
 259            if isinstance(rec, StringRec):
 260                fdict[tkey] = "STRING"
 261                fdict[vkey] = rec.rec
 262            if isinstance(rec, DateRec):
 263                fdict[tkey] = "DATE"
 264                fdict[vkey] = rec.rec
 265            if isinstance(rec, MultiRec):
 266                fdict[tkey] = "MULTI"
 267                fdict[vkey] = delim_cmp.join(rec.rec)
 268            if isinstance(rec, CatalogRec):
 269                fdict[tkey] = "CATALOG"
 270                fdict[vkey] = delim_cmp.join(rec.rec)
 271                
 272        fdicts.append(fdict)
 273    with open(outfile, "w", newline="") as f:
 274        writer = csv.DictWriter(f, fieldnames=colnames, delimiter=delim)
 275        writer.writeheader()
 276        for fdict in fdicts:
 277            writer.writerow(fdict)
 278    return outfile
 279
 280class traction:
 281    def __init__(self, target):
 282        """
 283         __init__ takes the db target either as string or dbcq object.
 284        """
 285        self.settings = cnf.makeload(path=".traction/settings.yaml", root=cnf.home, fmt="yaml", make=cnftemplate)        
 286        if self.settings is None:
 287            raise Exception("no settings.")
 288            return
 289        if isinstance(target, str):
 290            self.db = dbcq(target)
 291        elif isinstance(target, dbcq): 
 292            self.db = target
 293        else:
 294            raise Exception("target needs to be string or dbcq instance")
 295        self._idcinit()
 296        #print(f"_idcoids: {self._idcoids}")
 297        self.jd = {
 298            "sample_to_samplekit": [f"left join centraxx_samplekititem as samplekititem on samplekititem.tubebarcode = idc_{self.sidc()}.psn", "left join centraxx_samplekit as samplekit on samplekit.oid = samplekititem.samplekit"],
 299            "sample_to_parentid": [f"left join centraxx_sampleidcontainer parentidc on parentidc.sample = sample.parent and parentidc.idcontainertype={self._idcoid[self.sidc()]}"],
 300            "sample_to_samplelocation": ["left join centraxx_samplelocation samplelocation on samplelocation.oid = sample.samplelocation"],
 301            "sample_to_sampletype": ["left join centraxx_sampletype as sampletype on sampletype.oid = sample.sampletype"],
 302            "sample_to_stockprocessing": ["left join centraxx_stockprocessing as stockprocessing on sample.stockprocessing = stockprocessing.oid"],
 303            "sample_to_secondprocessing": ["left join centraxx_stockprocessing as secondprocessing on sample.secondprocessing = secondprocessing.oid"],
 304            "sample_to_project": ["left join centraxx_project as project on sample.project = project.oid"],
 305            "sample_to_patient": ["left join centraxx_patientcontainer as patientcontainer on sample.patientcontainer = patientcontainer.oid"],
 306            "sample_to_receptacle": ["left join centraxx_samplereceptable as receptable on sample.receptable = receptable.oid"], # receptable seems to be a typo in the table naming
 307            "sample_to_orga": ["left join centraxx_organisationunit as organisationunit on sample.orgunit = organisationunit.oid"],
 308            "sample_to_trial": ["left join centraxx_flexistudy as flexistudy on sample.flexistudy = flexistudy.oid"]
 309            ,
 310            "patient_to_orga": ["left join centraxx_patientorgunit patientorgunit on patientcontainer.oid=patientorgunit.patientcontainer_oid", "left join centraxx_organisationunit organisationunit on patientorgunit.orgunit_oid=organisationunit.oid"],
 311            "patient_to_trial": ["left join centraxx_patientstudy as patientstudy on patientstudy.patientcontainer = patientcontainer.oid", "left join centraxx_flexistudy as flexistudy on flexistudy.oid = patientstudy.flexistudy"],
 312            "patient_to_sample": ["left join centraxx_sample sample on sample.patientcontainer = patientcontainer.oid"]
 313            ,
 314            "participant_to_address": ["left join centraxx_participantaddress participantaddress on participantaddress.participant = participant.oid", "left join centraxx_address address on address.oid = participantaddress.oid"],
 315            "participant_to_credential": ["left join centraxx_credential credential on credential.participant = participant.oid"]
 316        }
 317        self.names_labval = None
 318        self.names_catalogentry = None
 319        self.names_usageentry = None
 320
 321    def sample(self, sampleids:list|None=None, oids:list|None=None, idc:dict|None=None, patientids:list|None=None, pidc:str|None=None, parentids:list|None=None, parentoids:list|None=None, locationpaths:list|None=None, locationnames:list|None=None, trials:list|None=None, kitids:list|None=None, cxxkitids:list|None=None, categories:list|None=None, types:list|None=None, orgas:list|None=None, samplingdates:list|None=None, receiptdates:list|None=None, derivaldates:list|None=None, first_repositiondates:list|None=None, repositiondates:list|None=None, stockprocessingdates:list|None=None, secondprocessingdates:list|None=None, files:dict|None=None, verbose:list|None=None, verbose_all:bool=False, primaryref:bool=False, incl_parents:bool=False, incl_childs:bool=False, incl_tree:bool=False, like:list|None=None, missing=False, order_by:str|None=None, top:int|None=None, by=None, print_query:bool=False, raw:bool=False):
 322        """
 323         sample gets sample(s) and returns them as a list of Sample instances.
 324         
 325         pass sampleids and other values to filter for as lists of strings,
 326         e.g. `sampleids=["a", "b", "c"]`.
 327         
 328         pass idcontainer lists to idc, like `idc={"extsampleid": ["a", "b", "c"]}`.
 329         
 330         pass fields that should be joined in into the verbose array, e.g.
 331         `verbose=[tr.locationpath]`. to join in everything, say
 332         `verbose_all=True`. this is slower than non-verbose.
 333         
 334         pass dates as a tuple of from and to datetime, e.g. `samplingdates=(datefrom, None)`.
 335         
 336         to match via like as opposed to exact, pass the respective tr
 337         constants or idcs to like, e.g. `like=[tr.locationpath]`.
 338         
 339         pass `by` to key returned samples by that tr constant or idc. if
 340         missing is also passed, only missings are returned.
 341        """
 342        if verbose is None:
 343            verbose = []
 344        if like is None:
 345            like = []
 346        if files is None:
 347            files = {}
 348        if idc is None:
 349            idc = {}
 350        vaa = [cxxkitid, kitid, locationname, locationpath, orga, parentid, 
 351               project, receptacle, type,
 352               secondprocessing, stockprocessing, trial]
 353        vaa.extend(self._pidcs(pidc))
 354        vaa.extend(self._sidcs())                
 355        if self.sidc() not in verbose:
 356            verbose.insert(0, self.sidc())
 357        if verbose_all:
 358            verbose = vaa
 359        verbose = self._concrete_idcs(verbose, pidc=pidc)
 360        files = self._concrete_idcs_dict(files, pidc=pidc)        
 361        if not _checkverbose(verbose, vaa): 
 362            raise Exception(f"verbose keys {verbose} need to be in {vaa}.")
 363        if incl_parents or incl_childs or incl_tree or primaryref:
 364            verbosepass = []
 365            if primaryref:
 366                verbosepass = verbose
 367            res = self.sample(sampleids=sampleids, idc=idc, parentids=parentids, parentoids=parentoids, patientids=patientids, pidc=pidc, trials=trials, locationpaths=locationpaths, kitids=kitids, cxxkitids=cxxkitids, categories=categories, samplingdates=samplingdates, receiptdates=receiptdates, derivaldates=derivaldates, first_repositiondates=first_repositiondates, repositiondates=repositiondates, stockprocessingdates=stockprocessingdates, secondprocessingdates=secondprocessingdates, verbose=verbosepass, verbose_all=False, like=like, missing=missing, order_by=order_by, top=top, print_query=print_query)
 368            if primaryref:
 369                for sample in res:
 370                    self._fill_in_primary(sample)
 371                return res
 372            
 373            s_oids = get_ids(res, "oid")
 374            withincl = []
 375            for s_oid in s_oids:
 376               if incl_parents:
 377                   p_oids = []
 378                   self._get_parents(s_oid, p_oids)
 379                   withincl.extend(p_oids)
 380                   withincl.append(s_oid)
 381               if incl_childs:
 382                   c_oids = []
 383                   self._get_childs(s_oid, c_oids)
 384                   withincl.append(s_oid)
 385                   withincl.extend(c_oids)
 386               if incl_tree:
 387                   p_oids = []
 388                   self._get_parents(s_oid, p_oids)
 389                   if len(p_oids) > 0:
 390                       root = p_oids[0]
 391                   else:
 392                       root = s_oid
 393                   c_oids = []
 394                   self._get_childs(root, c_oids)
 395                   withincl.append(root)
 396                   withincl.extend(c_oids)
 397            withincl = list(dict.fromkeys(withincl))
 398            return self.sample(oids=withincl, verbose=verbose, print_query=print_query, raw=raw) # todo pass verbose_all?
 399        lists = {
 400          sampleoid: oids,
 401          parentid: parentids,
 402          parentoid: parentoids,
 403          locationpath: locationpaths,
 404          locationname: locationnames,
 405          trial: trials,
 406          kitid: kitids,
 407          cxxkitid: cxxkitids,
 408          category: categories,
 409          orga: orgas,
 410          samplingdate: samplingdates,
 411          receiptdate: receiptdates,
 412          derivaldate: derivaldates,
 413          first_repositiondate: first_repositiondates,
 414          repositiondate: repositiondates,
 415          stockprocessingdate: stockprocessingdates,
 416          secondprocessingdate: secondprocessingdates,
 417          type: types
 418        }
 419        _dextend(idc, self.sidc(), sampleids)
 420        _dextend(idc, self.pidc(pidc), patientids)
 421        alllists = self._makealllists(lists, idc, files)
 422        (nontable, table) = self._makemove(alllists, 50)
 423        jselects = {
 424            self.sidc(): [f"idc_{self.sidc()}.psn as '{sampleid}'"],
 425            self.pidc(pidc): [f"idc_{self.pidc(pidc)}.psn as '{patientid}'"],                   
 426            cxxkitid: [f"samplekit.cxxkitid as '{cxxkitid}'"],
 427            #sampleid: [f"sidc.psn as '{sampleid}'"],
 428            parentid: [f"parentidc.psn as '{parentid}'"],
 429            kitid: [f"samplekit.kitid as '{kitid}'"],
 430            locationname: [f"samplelocation.locationid as '{locationname}'"], 
 431            locationpath: [f"samplelocation.locationpath as '{locationpath}'"],
 432            type: [f"sampletype.code as '{type}'"], # is there a type field already?
 433            stockprocessing: [f"stockprocessing.code as '{stockprocessing}'"],
 434            secondprocessing: [f"secondprocessing.code as '{secondprocessing}'"],
 435            project: [f"project.code as '{project}'"],
 436            #patientid: [f"patidc.psn as '{patientid}'"],
 437            receptacle: [f"receptable.code as '{receptacle}'"],
 438            orga: [f"organisationunit.code as '{orga}'"],
 439            trial: [f"flexistudy.code as '{trial}'"],
 440        }
 441        lselects = [
 442          f"sample.amountrest as {restamount}",
 443          f"sample.appointmentnumber as {appointment}",
 444          f"sample.dtype as {category}",
 445          f"sample.oid as {sampleoid}",
 446          f"sample.parent as {parentoid}",
 447          "sample.*"
 448        ]
 449        selectstr = self._selectstr(jselects, lselects, nontable, table, verbose)
 450        joins = {
 451            cxxkitid: self.jd["sample_to_samplekit"],
 452            parentid: self.jd["sample_to_parentid"],
 453            kitid: self.jd["sample_to_samplekit"],
 454            locationname: self.jd["sample_to_samplelocation"],
 455            locationpath: self.jd["sample_to_samplelocation"],
 456            type: self.jd["sample_to_sampletype"],
 457            stockprocessing: self.jd["sample_to_stockprocessing"],
 458            secondprocessing: self.jd["sample_to_secondprocessing"],
 459            project: self.jd["sample_to_project"],
 460            receptacle: self.jd["sample_to_receptacle"],
 461            orga: self.jd["sample_to_orga"],
 462            trial: self.jd["sample_to_trial"]
 463        }
 464        for pidc in self._pidcs(pidc):
 465            joins[pidc] = self.jd["sample_to_patient"]
 466        joinstr = self._joinstr(joins, nontable, table, verbose, pidc=pidc)
 467        (wherestr, whereargs) = self._where(nontable, table, like=like)
 468        topstr = self._top(top)
 469        query = f"select {topstr} {selectstr} from centraxx_sample sample \n{joinstr}"
 470        if wherestr.strip() != "":
 471            query += f"\nwhere {wherestr}"
 472        if order_by is not None:
 473            query += " " + self._order_by(order_by)
 474            
 475        if print_query:
 476           print(query)
 477           print(whereargs)
 478
 479        res = self.db.qfad(query, whereargs)
 480        self._cleartt(table["nonidc"])
 481        self._cleartt(table["idc"])
 482        if raw:
 483            return res
 484        if missing and by is None:
 485            by = _byifone(alllists)
 486        bydict, missinglst = _prepby(alllists, by, missing)
 487        sarr = []
 488        for r in res:
 489            ids = []
 490            #if dig(r, sampleid) is not None:
 491            #    ids.append(Identifier(id=dig(r, sampleid), code=self.sidc()))
 492            for idc in self._sidcs():
 493                #print("idc:" + idc)
 494                #print("r:" + str(r))
 495                if idc.lower() in r and r[idc.lower()] is not None:
 496                    ids.append( Identifier(id=dig(r, idc.lower()), code=idc.upper()) )
 497            ids.append( Identifier(id=int(dig(r, sampleoid)), code="oid") ) # todo rename sampleoid?
 498            pids = []
 499            if r[parentoid] is not None:
 500                pids.append(Identifier(id=int(r[parentoid]), code="oid"))
 501            if dig(r, parentid) is not None:
 502                pids.append(Identifier(id=dig(r, parentid), code=self.sidc()))
 503            parent = None
 504            if len(pids) > 0:
 505                parent = Idable(ids=pids, mainidc=self.sidc())
 506            patids = []
 507            for idc in self._pidcs(pidc):
 508                if idc.lower() in r and r[idc.lower()] is not None:
 509                    patids.append( Identifier(id=dig(r, idc.lower()), code=idc.upper()) )
 510
 511            patient = None
 512            if len(patids) > 0:
 513                patient = Idable(ids=patids, mainidc=self.pidc(pidc))
 514            s = Sample(
 515                appointment=dig(r, appointment),
 516                category=dig(r, category),
 517                samplingdate=dig(r, samplingdate),
 518                concentration=dig(r, concentration),
 519                cxxkitid=dig(r, cxxkitid),
 520                creationdate=dig(r, creationdate),
 521                derivaldate=dig(r, derivaldate),
 522                ids=Idable(ids=ids, mainidc=self.sidc()),
 523                initialamount=Amount(floatornull(dig(r, initialamount)), dig(r, initialunit)), # apparently the cast to float is explicitly needed
 524                kitid=dig(r, kitid),
 525                locationpath=dig(r, locationpath),
 526                locationname=dig(r, locationname),
 527                orga=dig(r, orga),
 528                parent=parent,
 529                patient=patient,
 530                project=dig(r, project),
 531                receiptdate=dig(r, receiptdate),
 532                receptacle=dig(r, receptacle),
 533                repositiondate=dig(r, repositiondate),
 534                restamount=Amount(floatornull(dig(r, restamount)), dig(r, restunit)),
 535                secondprocessing=dig(r, secondprocessing),
 536                secondprocessingdate=dig(r, secondprocessingdate),
 537                stockprocessing=dig(r, stockprocessing),
 538                stockprocessingdate=dig(r, stockprocessingdate),
 539                trial=dig(r, trial),
 540                type=dig(r, type), 
 541                xposition=dig(r, xposition), 
 542                yposition=dig(r, yposition)
 543            )
 544            if by is not None:
 545                if missing:
 546                    _updatemissing(missinglst, by, r)
 547                _fillby(bydict, by, r, s)
 548            else:
 549                sarr.append(s)
 550        if by is not None:
 551            if missing:
 552                return missinglst
 553            else:
 554                return bydict
 555        else:
 556            return sarr
 557    def _get_parents(self, oid, out):
 558        """
 559         _get_parents collects the parent sample oids of a sample in the `out`
 560         list, in order from root to leaf. take oids to include aliquotgroups,
 561         they don't necessarily have an idcontainer attached to them.
 562        """
 563        res = self.db.qfad("select parent from centraxx_sample where oid = ?", oid)
 564        pid = dig(res[0], "parent")
 565        if pid is not None:
 566            out.insert(0, pid)
 567            self._get_parents(pid, out)
 568    def _get_childs(self, oid, out):
 569        """
 570         _get_childs collects the oids of the sample's children in the `out`
 571         list, in order from root-to-leaf. take oids to include aliquotgroups.
 572        """
 573        res = self.db.qfad("select oid from centraxx_sample where parent = ?", oid)
 574        for child in res:
 575            out.append(child["oid"])
 576            self._get_childs(child["oid"], out)
 577    def patient(self, patientids:list|None=None, pidc:str|None=None, sampleids:list|None=None, idc:dict|None=None, trials:list|None=None, orgas:list|None=None, files:dict|None=None, verbose:list|None=None, verbose_all:bool=False, like:list|None=None, order_by:str|None=None, top:int|None=None, by:str=None, missing:bool=False, print_query:bool=False, raw:bool=False):
 578        """
 579         patient gets patients and returns them as a list of Patient instances.
 580         
 581         the parameters work analog to the sample method.
 582        """
 583        if verbose is None:
 584            verbose = []
 585        if like is None:
 586            like = []
 587        if files is None:
 588            files = {}
 589        if idc is None:
 590            idc = {}
 591        vaa = [orga]
 592        vaa.extend(self._pidcs(pidc))        
 593        if self.pidc(pidc) not in verbose:
 594            verbose.insert(0, self.pidc(pidc))
 595        verbose = self._concrete_idcs(verbose, pidc=pidc)
 596        files = self._concrete_idcs_dict(files, pidc=pidc)
 597        lists = {
 598          trial: trials,
 599          orga: orgas
 600        }
 601        _dextend(idc, self.sidc(), sampleids)
 602        _dextend(idc, self.pidc(pidc), patientids)
 603        alllists = self._makealllists(lists, idc, files)
 604        (nontable, table) = self._makemove(alllists, 50)
 605        if verbose_all:
 606            verbose = vaa
 607        if not _checkverbose(verbose, vaa):
 608            return None # throw error?
 609        selects = {
 610            self.pidc(pidc): [f"idc_{self.pidc(pidc)}.psn as '{patientid}'"],
 611            orga: [f"organisationunit.code as '{orga}'"],
 612            trial: [f"flexistudy.code as '{trial}'"],
 613        }
 614        joins = {
 615            orga: self.jd["patient_to_orga"],
 616            trial: self.jd["patient_to_trial"]
 617        }
 618        for sidc in self._sidcs():
 619            joins[sidc] = self.jd["patient_to_sample"]
 620        selectstr = self._selectstr(selects, ["patientcontainer.*"], nontable, table, verbose, pidc=pidc)
 621        joinstr = self._joinstr(joins, nontable, table, verbose, pidc=pidc)  
 622        (wherestr, whereargs) = self._where(nontable, table, like=like)
 623        #print(whereargs)
 624        topstr = self._top(top)
 625        query = f"select distinct {topstr} {selectstr} from centraxx_patientcontainer patientcontainer \n{joinstr}"
 626        if wherestr.strip() != "":
 627            query += f"\nwhere {wherestr}"
 628        if order_by is not None:
 629            query += " " + self._order_by(order_by)
 630        if print_query:
 631           print(query)
 632        res = self.db.qfad(query, whereargs)
 633        self._cleartt(table["nonidc"])
 634        self._cleartt(table["idc"])        
 635        if raw:
 636            return res
 637        if missing and by is None:
 638            by = _byifone(alllists)
 639        bydict, missinglst = _prepby(alllists, by, missing)
 640        pats = []
 641        for r in res:
 642            ids = []
 643            for idc in self._pidcs(pidc):
 644                if idc.lower() in r and r[idc.lower()] is not None:
 645                    ids.append( Identifier(id=dig(r, idc.lower()), code=idc) )
 646            pat = Patient(
 647              ids=Idable(ids=ids, mainidc=self.pidc(pidc)),
 648              orga=dig(r, orga)
 649            )
 650            if by is not None:
 651                if missing:
 652                    _updatemissing(missinglst, by, r)
 653                _fillby(bydict, by, r, pat)
 654            else:
 655                pats.append(pat)
 656        if by is not None:
 657            if missing:
 658                return missinglst
 659            else:
 660                return bydict
 661        else:
 662            return pats
 663    def trial(self):
 664        """
 665         trial gives trials.
 666        """
 667        query = "select code from centraxx_flexistudy"
 668        res = self.db.qfad(query)
 669        return res
 670    def finding(self, sampleids:list|None=None, patientids:list|None=None, pidc:str|None=None, idc:dict|None=None, methods:list|None=None, trials:list|None=None, values:bool=True, verbose:list|None=None, files:dict|None=None, verbose_all:bool=False, names:bool=False, top:int|None=None, print_query:bool=False, raw:bool=False):
 671        """
 672         finding gets the laborfindings ("messbefund" / "begleitschein") for
 673         sampleids or method.  it returns a list of Finding instances.
 674         
 675         you can pass these to verbose: tr.patientid  // maybe also tr.values?
 676        """
 677        if verbose is None:
 678            verbose = []
 679        if files is None:
 680            files = {}
 681        if idc is None:
 682            idc = {}
 683        vaa = [patientid] # include trial?
 684        if verbose_all:
 685            verbose = vaa
 686        if self.sidc() not in verbose:
 687            verbose.append(self.sidc())
 688        verbose = self._concrete_idcs(verbose, pidc=pidc)
 689        files = self._concrete_idcs_dict(files, pidc=pidc)
 690        lists = {
 691          method: methods,
 692          trial: trials
 693        }
 694        _dextend(idc, self.sidc(), sampleids)
 695        _dextend(idc, self.pidc(pidc), patientids)
 696        alllists = self._makealllists(lists, idc, files)
 697        (nontable, table) = self._makemove(alllists, 50)
 698        selects = {
 699            self.sidc(): [f"idc_{self.sidc()}.psn as '{sampleid}'"],
 700            self.pidc(pidc): [f"idc_{self.pidc(pidc)}.psn as '{patientid}'"],                   
 701        }
 702        idcselectstr = self._selectstr(selects, [], nontable, table, verbose, pidc=pidc)  
 703        joins = {
 704            trial: self.jd["sample_to_trial"]
 705        }
 706        for pidc in self._pidcs(pidc):
 707            joins[pidc] = self.jd["sample_to_patient"]
 708        idcjoinstr = self._joinstr(joins, nontable, table, verbose, pidc=pidc)
 709        topstr = self._top(top)
 710        query = f"""select {topstr} laborfinding.oid as "laborfinding_oid", laborfinding.*, labormethod.code as {method}, {idcselectstr}
 711        from centraxx_laborfinding as laborfinding
 712
 713        -- go from laborfinding to sample
 714        left join centraxx_labormethod as labormethod on laborfinding.labormethod = labormethod.oid
 715        left join centraxx_labormapping as labormapping on labormapping.laborfinding = laborfinding.oid
 716        left join centraxx_sample sample on labormapping.relatedoid = sample.oid
 717        {idcjoinstr}"""
 718        (wherestr, whereargs) = self._where(nontable, table)
 719        if wherestr.strip() != "":
 720            query += f"\nwhere {wherestr}"
 721            
 722        if print_query:
 723            print(query)
 724            print(whereargs)
 725        results = self.db.qfad(query, whereargs)
 726        self._cleartt(table["nonidc"])
 727        self._cleartt(table["idc"])        
 728        if names is True:
 729            self.names_laborvalue = self.name(table="laborvalue")
 730        for i, finding in enumerate(results):
 731            if not values: # todo put this outside of the loop?
 732                continue
 733            query = """select recordedvalue.*, laborvalue.code as laborvalue_code, laborvalue.dtype as laborvalue_type, laborvalue.custom_catalog as laborvalue_catalog_oid, unit.code as laborvalue_unit
 734                from centraxx_laborfinding as laborfinding
 735
 736                -- go from laborfinding to recorded value
 737                join centraxx_labfindinglabval as labfindinglabval on labfindinglabval.laborfinding = laborfinding.oid
 738                join centraxx_recordedvalue as recordedvalue on labfindinglabval.oid = recordedvalue.oid"""
 739            if self.cxx() == "3":
 740                query += """--go directly to laborvalue
 741                join centraxx_laborvalue laborvalue on labfindinglabval.laborvalue = laborvalue.oid"""
 742            elif self.cxx() == "4":
 743                query += """                
 744                -- go to laborvalue via crftempfield
 745                join centraxx_crftempfield crftempfield on labfindinglabval.crftempfield = crftempfield.oid
 746                join centraxx_laborvalue laborvalue on crftempfield.laborvalue = laborvalue.oid
 747                """
 748            query += """
 749                --go from laborvalue to unit
 750                left join centraxx_unity unit on laborvalue.unit = unit.oid
 751
 752                where laborfinding.oid = ?
 753            """
 754            recvals = self.db.qfad(query, finding['laborfinding_oid'])
 755            valsbycode = {}
 756            for recval in recvals:
 757              valsbycode[recval["laborvalue_code"]] = self._make_rec(recval, finding, names)
 758            results[i]["values"] = valsbycode
 759        if raw:
 760            return results
 761        findings = []
 762        for res in results:
 763            patids = []
 764            for idc in self._pidcs(pidc):
 765                if idc.lower() in res and res[idc.lower()] is not None:
 766                    patids.append( Identifier(id=dig(res, idc.lower()), code=idc.upper()) )
 767
 768            patient = None
 769            if len(patids) > 0:
 770                patient = Idable(ids=patids, mainidc=self.pidc(pidc))
 771            finding = Finding(
 772                findingdate=dig(res, "findingdate"),
 773                method=res["method"],
 774                name=res["shortname"],
 775                patient=patient,
 776                recs=res["values"] if "values" in res else None, # todo None ok?
 777                sample=Idable(id=res[sampleid], code=self.sidc(), mainidc=self.sidc()),
 778                sender=None
 779            )
 780            findings.append(finding)
 781        return findings
 782    def method(self, methods:list|None=None, files:dict|None=None):
 783        """
 784         method (messprofil) gets method(s) and their labvals (messparameter).
 785        """
 786        if files is None:
 787            files = {}
 788        lists = {
 789          method: methods
 790        }
 791        alllists = self._makealllists(lists, {}, files)
 792        (nontable, table) = self._makemove(alllists, 50)
 793        query = """select laborvalue.code as labval, labormethod.code as "method", laborvalue.dtype as labval_type, catalog.code as catalog
 794from centraxx_labormethod labormethod
 795inner join centraxx_crftemplate crf_t
 796    on labormethod.crf_template=crf_t.oid
 797inner join centraxx_crftempsection crf_ts
 798    on crf_t.oid=crf_ts.crftemplate
 799inner join centraxx_crftempsection_fields crf_tsf
 800    on crf_ts.oid=crf_tsf.crftempsection_oid
 801inner join centraxx_crftempfield crf_tf
 802    on crf_tsf.crftempfield_oid=crf_tf.oid
 803inner join centraxx_laborvalue laborvalue
 804    on crf_tf.laborvalue=laborvalue.oid
 805left join centraxx_catalog catalog
 806    on catalog.oid = laborvalue.custom_catalog"""
 807        (wherestr, whereargs) = self._where(nontable, table)
 808
 809        if wherestr and wherestr.strip != "()":
 810          query += " where " + wherestr
 811        # print(query)
 812        res = self.db.qfad(query, whereargs)
 813        self._cleartt(table["nonidc"])
 814        self._cleartt(table["idc"])
 815        methodnames = self.name(table="labormethod")
 816        labvalnames = self.name(table="laborvalue")
 817        out = {}
 818        for row in res:
 819            methodcode = row["method"]
 820            if methodcode not in out:
 821                out[methodcode] = {}
 822                out[methodcode]["code"] = methodcode
 823                out[methodcode]["name_de"] = dig(methodnames, methodcode + "/de")
 824                out[methodcode]["name_en"] = dig(methodnames, methodcode + "/en")
 825                out[methodcode]["labvals"] = {}
 826            labvalcode = row["labval"]
 827
 828            labval = {}
 829            labval["code"] = labvalcode
 830            labval["name_de"] = dig(labvalnames, labvalcode + "/de")
 831            labval["name_en"] = dig(labvalnames, labvalcode + "/en")
 832            if dig(row, "catalog") is not None:
 833                labval["catalog"] = dig(row, "catalog")
 834            labval["type"] = row["labval_type"]
 835            if labval["type"] == "OPTIONGROUP" or labval["type"] == "ENUMERATION":
 836                labval["usageentry"] = self.usageentry(labvals=[labval])
 837            out[methodcode]["labvals"][labvalcode] = labval
 838        return out
 839    def user(self, usernames:list|None=None, emails:list|None=None, lastlogins:list|None=None, files:dict|None=None, verbose:list|None=None, top:int|None=None, verbose_all:bool=False, print_query:bool=False):
 840        """
 841        """
 842        if verbose is None:
 843            verbose = []
 844        if files is None:
 845            files = {}
 846        vaa = [address, login]
 847        if verbose_all:
 848            verbose = vaa
 849        lists = {
 850          username: usernames,
 851          address: emails,
 852          login: lastlogins
 853        }
 854        alllists = self._makealllists(lists, {}, files)
 855        (nontable, table) = self._makemove(alllists, 50)
 856        jselects = {
 857            address: [f"address.email as email"],
 858            login: [f"credential.last_login_on as lastlogin"]
 859        }
 860        lselects = [
 861            f"participant.username as {username}",
 862            f"participant.firstname as firstname",
 863            f"participant.lastname as lastname"
 864        ]
 865        selectstr = self._selectstr(jselects, lselects, nontable, table, verbose)
 866        joins = {
 867            address: self.jd["participant_to_address"],
 868            login: self.jd["participant_to_credential"]
 869        }
 870        joinstr = self._joinstr(joins, nontable, table, verbose)
 871        (wherestr, whereargs) = self._where(nontable, table, like=[])
 872        topstr = self._top(top)
 873        query = f"select {topstr} {selectstr} from centraxx_participant participant\n{joinstr}"
 874        if wherestr.strip() != "":
 875            query += f"\nwhere {wherestr}"
 876        if print_query:
 877            print(query)
 878            print(whereargs)
 879        res = self.db.qfad(query, whereargs)
 880        self._cleartt(table["nonidc"])
 881        self._cleartt(table["idc"])        
 882        out = []
 883        for r in res:
 884            user = User(
 885                email=dig(r, email),
 886                lastlogin=dig(r, lastlogin),
 887                username=dig(r, username),
 888                firstname=dig(r, "firstname"),
 889                lastname=dig(r, "lastname")
 890            )
 891            out.append(user)
 892        return out
 893    def orga(self, names:bool=False):
 894        """
 895         orga returns organisationunits.
 896        """
 897        query = "select code from centraxx_organisationunit"
 898        res = self.db.qfad(query)
 899        names = self.name(table="organisationunit", ml_table="organisatunit_ml_name")
 900        for i, _ in enumerate(res):
 901            nam = names[res[i]["code"]]
 902            res[i]["name_de"] = nam["de"]
 903            res[i]["name_en"] = nam["en"]
 904        return res
 905    def catalog(self, catalogs:list|None=None, files:dict|None=None):
 906        """
 907         catalog gives the catalogentries per catalog.
 908        """
 909        if files is None:
 910            files = {}
 911        lists = {
 912            catalog: catalogs
 913        }
 914        alllists = self._makealllists(lists, {}, files)
 915        (nontable, table) = self._makemove(alllists, 50)
 916        query = """select catalogentry.code as 'entry_code', catalog.code as 'catalog_code' from centraxx_catalogentry catalogentry
 917join centraxx_catalog catalog on catalogentry.catalog = catalog.oid"""
 918        (wherestr, whereargs) = self._where(nontable, table) 
 919        if wherestr and wherestr.strip() != "()":
 920            query += f"\nwhere {wherestr}"
 921        res = self.db.qfad(query, whereargs)
 922        self._cleartt(table["nonidc"])
 923        self._cleartt(table["idc"])
 924        catnames = self.name(table="catalog")
 925        entrynames = self.name(table="catalogentry")
 926        out = {}
 927        for row in res:
 928            entrycode = dig(row, "entry_code")
 929            catcode = dig(row, "catalog_code")
 930            # create the catalog
 931            if catcode not in out:
 932                out[catcode] = {}
 933                out[catcode]["code"] = catcode
 934                out[catcode]["name_de"] = dig(catnames, catcode + "/de")
 935                out[catcode]["name_en"] = dig(catnames, catcode + "/en")
 936                out[catcode]["entries"] = {}
 937            # add the entry
 938            entry = {}
 939            entry["code"] = entrycode
 940            entry["name_de"] = dig(entrynames, entrycode + "/de")
 941            entry["name_en"] = dig(entrynames, entrycode + "/en")        
 942            out[catcode]["entries"][entrycode] = entry
 943        return out
 944    def usageentry(self, labvals:list=None):
 945        """
 946         usageentry gives the usageentries.
 947        """
 948        query = "select code from centraxx_usageentry"
 949        res = self.db.qfad(query)
 950        names = self.name(table="usageentry")
 951        out = {}
 952        for row in res:
 953            code = row["code"]
 954            out[code] = {}
 955            out[code]["name_de"] = dig(names, code + "/de")
 956            out[code]["name_en"] = dig(names, code + "/en")
 957            out[code]["code"] = code
 958        return out
 959    def name(self, table:str, code:str=None, lang:str=None, ml_table:str=None):
 960        """
 961         name gives the multilingual names for a code or all codes in a table.
 962         
 963         the result is keyed by code and language like this:
 964         
 965         
 966         "NUM_NMR_ISOLEUCINE_VALUE": {  
 967            "de": "Isoleucin",  
 968            "en": "Isoleucine"  
 969         }
 970         
 971         
 972         table: the name of the centraxx table without centraxx_ prefix  
 973         code: a specific code, if none given, all code - name mappings for table are given  
 974         lang: de|en  
 975         ml_table (cxx3): if the name of the table connecting to multilingualentry is not simlpy the queried table name followed by "_ml_name", give the connecting table's name here. eg: name('laborvaluegroup', ..., ml_name='labval_grp_ml_name')
 976        """
 977        if self.cxx() == "3":
 978            return self._name_cxx3(table, code, lang, ml_table)
 979        elif self.cxx() == "4":
 980            return self._name_cxx4(table, code, lang)
 981    def _name_cxx3(self, table:str, code:str=None, lang:str=None, ml_table:str=None):
 982        """
 983         _name_cxx3 gives display names for cxx 3.
 984        """
 985        if not isidentifier(table) or (ml_table is not None and not isidentifier(ml_table)):
 986            raise Exception(f"names {table} and {ml_table} need to be valid sql identifiers.")
 987        query = "select [" + table + "].code, multilingual.value as name, multilingual.lang as lang"
 988        query += " from [centraxx_" + table + "] as [" + table + "]"
 989        ml_name = ""
 990        if ml_table is not None: # the name is different
 991            ml_name = "centraxx_" + ml_table
 992        else: # the name is the same
 993            ml_name = "centraxx_" + table + "_ml_name"
 994        query += " inner join [" + ml_name + "] mlname on mlname.related_oid = [" + table + "].oid"
 995        query += " inner join centraxx_multilingualentry multilingual on mlname.oid = multilingual.oid"
 996        wherestrings = []
 997        args = []
 998        if code is not None:
 999            wherestrings.append(self._whereparam("[" + table + "].code"))
1000            args.append(code)
1001        if lang is not None:
1002            wherestrings.append(self._whereparam("multilingual.lang"))
1003            args.append(lang)
1004        if len(wherestrings) > 0:
1005            query += " where "
1006            # join where clauses by and
1007            query += " and ".join(wherestrings)
1008
1009        # print(query)
1010        res = self.db.qfad(query, *args)
1011        out = {}
1012        for line in res:
1013            code = line["code"]
1014            lang = line["lang"]
1015            if code not in out:
1016               out[code] = {}
1017            out[code][lang] = line["name"]
1018        return out
1019    def _name_cxx4(self, table:str, code:str=None, lang:str=None):
1020        """
1021         _name_cxx4 gives display names for cxx 4.
1022        """
1023        if not isidentifier(table):
1024            raise Exception(f"name {table} needs to be a valid sql identifier.")
1025        fieldname = table
1026        if table == "organisationunit":
1027            fieldname = "organizationunit"
1028        query = f"select lang, ml_name as name, {table}.code from centraxx_multilingual multilingual join centraxx_{table} {table} on {table}.oid = multilingual.{table} where multilingual.{fieldname} is not null"
1029        wherestrings = []
1030        args = []
1031        if code is not None:
1032            wherestrings.append(self._whereparam("[" + table + "].code"))
1033            args.append(code)
1034        if lang is not None:
1035            wherestrings.append(self._whereparam("multilingual.lang"))
1036            args.append(lang)
1037        if len(wherestrings) > 0:
1038            query += " where "
1039            # join where clauses by and
1040            query += " and ".join(wherestrings)
1041        res = self.db.qfad(query, *args)
1042        out = {}
1043        for line in res:
1044            code = line["code"]
1045            lang = line["lang"]
1046            if code not in out:
1047               out[code] = {}
1048            out[code][lang] = line["name"]
1049        return out
1050
1051    # query
1052    def _selectstr(self, selects, selecta, nontable, table, verbose, pidc:str=None):
1053        """
1054         _selectstr gives the selects for idc keys and verbose array. unlike
1055         _joinstr, keys in lists and tmptables aren't included automatically,
1056         cause they may be needed in where but are not necessarily interesting
1057         for the output (is that true?). selecta is for fields that should be
1058         selected regardless if they're in the verbose array or not.
1059         
1060         the idc argument assumes that the sample table is joined it. // todo is this still true?
1061        """
1062        (vnonidc, vidc) = self._splitidc(verbose, pidc=pidc)
1063        nonidc = self._collkeys(nontable["nonidc"], table["nonidc"], vnonidc)
1064        idc = self._collkeys(nontable["idc"], table["idc"], vidc)
1065        for key in nonidc:
1066            if key not in selects:
1067                continue
1068            for s in selects[key]:
1069                selecta.append(s)
1070        selecta = self._select_idc(selecta, idc)
1071        selectstr = ", \n".join(selecta)
1072        return selectstr
1073    def _select_idc(self, selecta, idca:list):
1074        """
1075         _select_idc adds the sql select statements for a list of idc keys.
1076        """
1077        for item in idca:
1078          selectstr = f"idc_{item}.psn as '{item}'"
1079          if selectstr not in selecta:
1080            selecta.append(selectstr)
1081        return selecta
1082    def _joinstr(self, joins, nontable, table, verbose, pidc:str|None=None):
1083        """
1084         _joinstr puts together the joins needed for the lists and temporary
1085         tables of non-idc and idc keys, and verbose. it returns the sql join
1086         string.
1087        """
1088        (vnonidc, vidc) = self._splitidc(verbose, pidc=pidc)
1089        nonidc = self._collkeys(nontable["nonidc"], table["nonidc"], vnonidc)
1090        idc = self._collkeys(nontable["idc"], table["idc"], vidc)
1091        joina = []
1092        joina = self._join_idc(joina, idc, joins)
1093        for key in nonidc: 
1094            if key not in joins:
1095                continue
1096            for s in joins[key]:
1097                if s not in joina:
1098                    joina.append(s)
1099        joinstr = " \n".join(joina)
1100        return joinstr
1101    def _join_idc(self, joina, idca:list, joins):
1102        """
1103         _join_idc adds the sql join statements for the idc keys of the query.
1104        """
1105        for item in idca:
1106          if item in joins:
1107            for s in joins[item]:
1108              if s not in joina:
1109                joina.append(s)
1110          if self._idckind[item] == "SAMPLE":
1111            joinstr = f"left join centraxx_sampleidcontainer as idc_{item} on idc_{item}.sample = sample.oid and idc_{item}.idcontainertype = {self._idcoid[item]}"
1112    
1113            if joinstr not in joina:
1114              joina.append(joinstr)
1115          elif self._idckind[item] == "PATIENT":
1116            joinstr = f"left join centraxx_idcontainer as idc_{item} on idc_{item}.patientcontainer = patientcontainer.oid and idc_{item}.idcontainertype = {self._idcoid[item]}"
1117            if joinstr not in joina: # neccessary?
1118              joina.append(joinstr)
1119          else:
1120            print(f"error: idcontainer kind {self._idckind[item]} not supported.")
1121        return joina
1122    def _where(self, nontable:dict, table:dict, like:list=None): # -> (str, list)
1123        """
1124         _where returns the wherestring and args array for the provided
1125         lists and temporary tables.
1126         
1127         the user needs to make sure that whatever is used here is joined
1128         into the query before.
1129         
1130         the keys in nontable["nonidc"] and table["nonidc"] are assumed to be
1131         the tr constants, e.g. tr.locationpath, the keys in nontable["idc"]
1132         and table["idc"] are assumed to be idcontainers.
1133         
1134         like holds tr constants and idcontainers for which to check likeness
1135         instead of equality.
1136        """
1137        if like is None:
1138            like = []
1139        wheredict = { 
1140          trial: { "field": "flexistudy.code" },
1141          locationpath: { "field": "samplelocation.locationpath" },
1142          locationname: { "field": "samplelocation.locationid" },          
1143          method: { "field": "labormethod.code" },
1144          kitid: { "field": "samplekit.kitid" },
1145          cxxkitid: { "field": "samplekit.cxxkitid" },
1146          catalog: { "field": "catalog.code" },
1147          category: { "field": "sample.dtype" },
1148          type: { "field": "sampletype.code" },          
1149          orga: { "field": "organisationunit.code" },
1150          parentid: { "field": "parentidc.psn" },
1151          parentoid: { "field": "sample.parent" },
1152          sampleoid: { "field": "sample.oid" },          
1153          samplingdate: { "field": "sample.samplingdate", "type": "date" },
1154          receiptdate: { "field": "sample.receiptdate", "type": "date" },
1155          derivaldate: { "field": "sample.derivaldate", "type": "date" },
1156          first_repositiondate: { "field": "sample.first_repositiondate", "type": "date" },
1157          repositiondate: { "field": "sample.repositiondate", "type": "date" },
1158          stockprocessingdate: { "field": "sample.stockprocessingdate", "type": "date" },
1159          secondprocessingdate: { "field": "sample.secondprocessingdate", "type": "date" },
1160          username: { "field": "participant.username" },
1161          address: { "field": "address.email" },
1162          login: { "field": "credential.last_login_on", "type": "date" }
1163        }
1164        wherestrs = []
1165        whereargs = []
1166        for key in _uniq(list(nontable["nonidc"].keys()) + list(table["nonidc"].keys())):
1167           if dig(nontable["nonidc"], key) is not None and dig(wheredict, key + "/type") == "date":
1168               (wstr, wargs) = self._wheredate(dig(nontable["nonidc"], key), wheredict[key]["field"])
1169           else:
1170               (wstr, wargs) = self._whereexact(dig(nontable["nonidc"], key), dig(table["nonidc"], key), wheredict[key]["field"])
1171           if wstr != "()":
1172               wherestrs.append(wstr)
1173               whereargs.extend(wargs)
1174        for key in _uniq(list(nontable["idc"].keys()) + list(table["idc"].keys())):
1175            (wstr, wargs) = self._whereexact(dig(nontable["idc"], key), dig(table["idc"], key), f"idc_{key}.psn")
1176            if wstr != "()":
1177                wherestrs.append(wstr)
1178                whereargs.extend(wargs)
1179        wherestr = " and ".join(wherestrs)
1180        return (wherestr, whereargs)
1181    def _whereexact(self, lst, tmptable, dbfield:str): # -> (str, list)
1182        """
1183         _whereexact returns a where clause and argument list for exact
1184         matching of items in list and temporary table. NULL string is list is
1185         treated as sql NULL value.
1186        """
1187        wherearg = []
1188        wherestr = "("
1189        needsor = False
1190        if lst is not None and 'NULL' in lst:
1191            lst.remove("NULL")
1192            wherestr += dbfield + " is NULL"
1193            needsor = True
1194        if lst is not None and len(lst) > 0:
1195            if needsor:
1196                wherestr += " or "
1197            placeholder = traction._sqlinplaceholder(len(lst)) # todo put in package? tr._sqlinplaceholder
1198            wherestr += dbfield + " in " + placeholder # e.g. samplelocation.locationpath in (?, ?, ?)
1199            wherearg.extend(lst)
1200            # if something comes next, chain it with or
1201            needsor = True
1202        if tmptable is not None:
1203            if needsor:
1204                wherestr += " or "
1205            wherestr += dbfield + " in "
1206            wherestr += f"(select stdin from tempdb..{tmptable})"
1207        wherestr += ")"
1208        return (wherestr, wherearg)
1209    def _wheredate(self, tpl, dbfield): # -> (str, list)
1210        """
1211         _wheredate makes a date check. for now only check that it's between
1212         the two values of the array passed as date parameter.
1213        """
1214        wstr = ""
1215        wargs = []
1216        if tpl == 'NULL':
1217            wstr = "(" + dbfield + " is NULL)"
1218        else:
1219            s = "(CAST(" + dbfield + " as date) between ISNULL(?, " + dbfield + ") and ISNULL(?, " + dbfield + "))"
1220            wstr = s
1221            wargs.extend(tpl)
1222        return (wstr, wargs)
1223    def _sqlinplaceholder(n):
1224        """
1225         _sqlinplaceholder returns a string like (?, ?, ?, ? ...) with n question marks for sql in.
1226        """
1227
1228        # put this in a package sqlutil?
1229
1230        out = "("
1231        for i in range(n):
1232            out += "?"
1233            if i < n - 1:
1234                out += ","
1235        out += ")"
1236        return out
1237    def _whereparam(self, name, like:bool=None):
1238        """
1239         _whereparam gives a ?-parameterized sql where expression for name
1240         equal or like parameter for use in queries.
1241        """
1242        if like is None or not like:
1243            return name + " = ?"
1244        else:
1245            return name + " like '%' + ? + '%'"
1246    def _wherelike(self, name):
1247        """
1248         _wherelike gives a ?-parameterized sql where like expression.
1249        """
1250        return name + " like '%' + ? + '%'"
1251
1252    def _wherelikes(self, field, n):
1253        """
1254         _wherelikes gives a ?-parameterized sql of or-joined where-like
1255         expressions, as many as given n.
1256        """
1257        a = []
1258        for i in range(n):
1259            a.append(field + " like '%' + ? + '%'") # the sql takes literal plusses like here
1260        return " or ".join(a)
1261    def _top(self, top):
1262        """
1263         _top returns an injection-safe top string, for, e.g. `select top 100 * from table`, if
1264         top is None return an empty string.
1265        """
1266        if top is not None:
1267           if not isnumber(top):
1268               raise Exception(f"top param {top} needs to be a number")
1269           return f"top {top}"
1270        return ""
1271    def _order_by(self, order_by):
1272        """
1273         _order_by returns an injection safe order by string.
1274        """
1275        if not isidentifier(order_by):
1276            raise Exception(f"order_by param {order_by} needs to be an sql identifier.")
1277        return f"order by {order_by}"
1278    def _idcinit(self):
1279        """
1280         _idcinit makes a mapping of idcontainers from their code (lower-case)
1281         to respective oid and kind, and makes a list containing all idcs
1282         including sidc and pidc.
1283        """
1284        query = "select code, oid, kind from centraxx_idcontainertype"
1285        res = self.db.qfad(query)
1286        self._idcoid = {}
1287        self._idckind = {}
1288        for row in res:
1289          self._idcoid[row["code"]] = row["oid"]
1290          self._idckind[row["code"]] = row["kind"]
1291        self._idcs = []
1292        for id in self.settings['idc'] + [self.sidc(), self.pidc()]:
1293            if id not in self._idcs:
1294                self._idcs.append(id)
1295    def _make_rec(self, recval, finding, names:bool=False) -> Rec:
1296        """
1297         _make_rec makes a recorded value instance for finding from db results with display names if wished.
1298        """
1299        out:Rec = None
1300        if recval["laborvalue_type"] == "BOOLEAN":
1301            val = True if recval["boolvalue"] == 1 else False
1302            out = BooleanRec(method=finding["method"], labval=recval["laborvalue_code"], rec=val)
1303        elif recval["laborvalue_type"] == "DECIMAL":
1304            #print(recval["laborvalue_code"])
1305            #print(recval)
1306            value = float(recval["numericvalue"]) if recval["numericvalue"] is not None else None
1307            out = NumberRec(method=finding["method"], labval=recval["laborvalue_code"], rec=value, unit=recval["laborvalue_unit"])
1308        elif recval["laborvalue_type"] == "STRING" or recval["laborvalue_type"] == "LONGSTRING":
1309            out = StringRec(method=finding["method"], labval=recval["laborvalue_code"], rec=recval["stringvalue"])
1310        elif recval["laborvalue_type"] == "DATE":
1311            out = DateRec(method=finding["method"], labval=recval["laborvalue_code"], rec=recval["datevalue"])
1312        elif recval["laborvalue_type"] == "LONGDATE":
1313            out = DateRec(method=finding["method"], labval=recval["laborvalue_code"], rec=recval["datevalueprecision"])
1314        elif recval["laborvalue_type"] == "CATALOG":
1315            # get the catalog code
1316            query = """select catalog.code as 'catalog_code' from centraxx_catalog as catalog
1317            where catalog.oid = ?""" # do this once for all catalogs on startup or finding() call?
1318            res = self.db.qfad(query, recval['laborvalue_catalog_oid'])
1319            catalog_code = res[0]["catalog_code"]
1320            # get the catalog entries
1321            query = """select catalogentry.code as 'catalogentry_code' from centraxx_recordedvalue as recordedvalue
1322            join centraxx_recordedval_catentry as recordedval_catentry on recordedval_catentry.recordedvalue_oid = recordedvalue.oid
1323            join centraxx_catalogentry as catalogentry on catalogentry.oid = recordedval_catentry.catalogentry_oid
1324            where recordedvalue.oid = ?"""
1325            res = self.db.qfad(query, recval['oid'])
1326            # get the catalogentry names if they are not already loaded, and cache them. maybe it's faster to load the whole names map once instead of joining them in each time?
1327            if names is True and self.names_catalogentry is None:
1328                self.names_catalogentry = self.name(table="catalogentry")
1329            entries = []
1330            value_name = {}
1331            for r in res:
1332                code = r["catalogentry_code"]
1333                entries.append(code)
1334                if names is True:
1335                    value_name[code] = self.names_catalogentry[code]
1336            
1337            out = CatalogRec(method=finding["method"], labval=recval["laborvalue_code"], catalog=catalog_code, rec=entries, rec_name=value_name)
1338        elif recval["laborvalue_type"] == "ENUMERATION" or recval["laborvalue_type"] == "OPTIONGROUP":
1339            query = """select usageentry.code as 'usageentry_code' from centraxx_recordedvalue as recordedvalue
1340            join centraxx_recordedval_usagentry as recordedval_usagentry on recordedval_usagentry.recordedvalue_oid = recordedvalue.oid
1341            join centraxx_usageentry as usageentry on usageentry.oid = recordedval_usagentry.usageentry_oid
1342            where recordedvalue.oid = ?"""
1343            res = self.db.qfad(query, recval['oid'])
1344
1345            # get the usageentry names if they are not already loaded, and cache them. maybe it's faster to load the whole names map once instead of joining them in each time?
1346            if names is True and self.names_usageentry is None:
1347                self.names_usageentry = self.name(table="usageentry")
1348
1349            entries = []
1350            value_name = {}
1351            for r in res:
1352                code = r["usageentry_code"]
1353                entries.append(code)
1354                if names is True:
1355                    value_name[code] = self.names_usageentry[code]
1356            
1357            out = MultiRec(method=finding["method"], labval=recval["laborvalue_code"], rec=entries, rec_name=value_name)
1358        else:
1359            raise Exception(f"no record class for laborvalue of type {recval['laborvalue_type']}")
1360        return out
1361    
1362    def _fill_in_primary(self, sample:Sample):
1363        """
1364         _fill_in_primary adds a reference to the given sample's primary sample,
1365         if there is one.
1366        """
1367        poids = []
1368        self._get_parents(sample.id("oid"), poids)
1369        if len(poids) > 0:
1370            primary_oid = poids[0]
1371            primary = self.sample(oids=[primary_oid])[0]
1372            sample.primary = Idable(ids=primary.ids, mainidc=primary.mainidc)
1373
1374    # lists and tmp tables
1375    def _makemove(self, alllists:dict, cutoff:int): # -> (dict, dict)#bm
1376        """
1377         _makemove makes temporary tables from the given lists that are longer
1378         than cutoff. it returns one dict holding the remaining, non-table
1379         lists and one dict holding the table names of the created temporary
1380         tables, both return dicts distinguishing between nonidc and idc.
1381        """
1382        nontables = {}
1383        (nontables["nonidc"], ttlists) = _tablelists(alllists["nonidc"], cutoff)
1384        (nontables["idc"], ttidclists) = _tablelists(alllists["idc"], cutoff)        
1385        tables = {}
1386        tables["nonidc"] = self._maketables(ttlists, "tmp_")
1387        tables["idc"] = self._maketables(ttidclists, "tmp_idc_")
1388        return (nontables, tables) 
1389    def _makealllists(self, lists, idc, files):
1390        """
1391         _makealllists reads the lists from files, puts them together with the
1392         lists passed as lists, and returns them all in one dict,
1393         keeping nonidc and idc seperate.
1394        """
1395        alllists = {}
1396        alllists["nonidc"] = {}
1397        alllists["idc"] = {}
1398        for key, lst in lists.items():
1399            if lst is not None:
1400                alllists["nonidc"][key] = lst
1401        for key, lst in idc.items():
1402            alllists["idc"][key] = lst
1403        (ff, ffidc) = self._readfiles(files)
1404        for key, lst in ff.items():
1405            if key not in alllists["nonidc"]:
1406                alllists["nonidc"][key] = []
1407            alllists["nonidc"][key].extend(lst)
1408        for key, lst in ffidc.items():
1409            if key not in alllists["idc"]:
1410                alllists["idc"][key] = []
1411            alllists["idc"][key].extend(lst)
1412        return alllists
1413    def _readfiles(self, files, sidc:str=None, pidc:str=None):
1414        """
1415         _readfiles reads the files from which tmp tables should be build into
1416         lists.  it returns a tuple of one dict holding the non-idc lists and
1417         one dict holding the idc lists.
1418        """
1419        both = {}
1420        for key, filepath in files.items():
1421            lst = []
1422            with open(filepath, "r") as f:
1423                for line in f:
1424                    lst.append(line.rstrip())
1425            both[key] = lst
1426        nonidc = {}
1427        idc = {}
1428        for key, _ in both.items():
1429            if key == sampleid:
1430                idc[self.sidc()] = both[key]
1431            elif key == patientid:
1432                idc[self.pidc(pidc)] = both[key]
1433            elif self._is_idc(key, sidc=sidc, pidc=pidc):
1434                idc[key] = both[key]
1435            else:
1436                nonidc[key] = both[key]
1437        return (nonidc, idc)
1438    def _maketables(self, d:dict, prefix:str=""): #-> dict
1439        """
1440         _maketables makes temporary tables for each key in the given dict,
1441         inserts the list of values belonging to that key, and returns a dict
1442         the table names by key.
1443        """
1444        out = {}
1445        for key, lst in d.items():
1446            #name = "tempdb..#" + prefix + key
1447            name = "#" + prefix + key
1448            if not isidentifier(name):
1449                raise Exception(f"name {name} needs to be a valid sql identifier.")
1450            self.db.query(f"create table {name} (stdin varchar (255) )")
1451            for e in lst:
1452                self.db.query(f"insert into {name} values (?)", e)
1453            out[key] = name
1454        return out
1455    def _cleartt(self, tmptables:dict):
1456        """
1457         _cleartt drops the temporary tables tables that were created with
1458         _maketables after the query using their data was run. it takes the
1459         dict of filetables that was returned by _maketables.
1460         
1461         they would be dropped automatically after the session, but in case
1462         several queries are sent in one session, maybe it's better to drop
1463         them after each query.
1464        """
1465        for key, tablename in tmptables.items():
1466            if not isidentifier(tablename):
1467                raise Exception("table name {tablename} needs to be a valid sql identifier.")
1468            q = f"drop table tempdb..{tablename}"
1469            #print(q)
1470            self.db.query(q)  # todo is kairos_spring used again after this?
1471
1472    # settings / idc related
1473    def sidc(self) -> str:
1474        """
1475         sidc returns the main idc code by which samples are referenced as
1476         specified in the settings. 
1477        """
1478        if self.db.target not in self.settings['sampleid']:
1479            raise Exception(f"please specify the main sample idcontainer for {self.db.target} in settings.yaml")
1480        return self.settings['sampleid'][self.db.target]
1481    def pidc(self, pidc:str|None=None) -> str:
1482        """
1483        """
1484        if pidc is not None:
1485            return pidc
1486        if self.db.target not in self.settings['patientid']:
1487            raise Exception(f"please specify the main patient idcontainer for {self.db.target} in settings.yaml")
1488        return self.settings['patientid'][self.db.target]
1489    def _sidcs(self) -> list:
1490        """
1491         _sidcs returns the idcs from settings that are specific for sample.
1492         
1493         rather make this a section in conf? but maybe not, keep the conf simple and let the program sort out pidc and sidc.
1494         
1495         # idc holds idcontainer codes that should be queryable as command line flags 
1496         idc:
1497           sample:
1498             - extsampleid
1499             - modul
1500             - tier
1501           patient:
1502             - mpi
1503        """
1504        out = []
1505        for idc in self.settings["idc"]:
1506            if idc in self._idckind and self._idckind[idc] == "SAMPLE" and not idc in out:
1507                out.append(idc)
1508        sidc = self.sidc()
1509        if sidc not in out:
1510            out.append(self.sidc())
1511        return out
1512    def _pidcs(self, pidc:str=None) -> list:
1513        """
1514         _pidcs returns the idcs from settings that are specific for
1515         sample.
1516        """
1517        out = []
1518        for idc in self.settings["idc"]:
1519            if idc in self._idckind and self._idckind[idc] == "PATIENT" and idc not in out:
1520                out.append(idc)
1521        pidc = self.pidc(pidc)
1522        if pidc not in out:
1523            out.append(pidc)
1524        return out
1525    def _concrete_idcs(self, verbose, pidc=None):
1526        """
1527         _concrete_idcs replaces traction constants patientid and sampleid with their
1528         respective idc for array.
1529        """
1530        out = []
1531        for verb in verbose:
1532            if verb == patientid:
1533                out.append(self.pidc(pidc))
1534            elif verb == sampleid:
1535                out.append(self.sidc())
1536            else:
1537                out.append(verb)
1538        return out
1539    def _concrete_idcs_dict(self, d, pidc=None):
1540        """
1541         _concrete_idcs_dict replaces traction constants patientid and sampleid with their
1542         respective idc for dict.
1543        """
1544        out = {}
1545        for key, val in d.items():
1546            #print("key: " + key)
1547            if key == patientid:
1548                out[self.pidc(pidc)] = val
1549            elif key == sampleid:
1550                #print("here: " + self.sidc())
1551                out[self.sidc()] = val
1552            else:
1553                out[key] = val
1554        return out
1555    def cxx(self) -> str:
1556        """
1557         cxx gives the centraxx version for db target from settings.
1558        """
1559        v = dig(self.settings, f"cxx/{self.db.target}")
1560        if v is None:
1561            return None
1562        return str(v)
1563
1564    def _is_idc(self, key:str, sidc:str=None, pidc:str=None): # -> bool
1565        """
1566         _is_idc says whether a string is an idcontainer mentioned in
1567         settings.yaml or passed as sidc or pidc argument.
1568        """
1569        return key in self.settings["idc"] or key == self.sidc() or key == self.pidc(pidc)
1570    def _splitidc(self, arr:list, pidc:str=None): # -> (list, list)
1571        """
1572         _splitidc splits an array of tr constants and idc keys into non-idc
1573         (tr constants) and idc keys.
1574        """
1575        nonidc = []
1576        idc = []
1577        for k in arr:
1578            if self._is_idc(k, pidc=pidc):
1579                idc.append(k)
1580            else:
1581                nonidc.append(k)
1582        return (nonidc, idc)
1583    def _collkeys(self, lists:dict=None, tmptables:dict=None, verbose:list=None):
1584        """
1585         _collkeys collects the keys join needs from lists, temporary tables
1586         and verbose. preserves order and ensures uniqueness.
1587        """
1588        if lists is None:
1589            lists = {}
1590        if tmptables is None:
1591            tmptables = {}
1592        if verbose is None:
1593            verbose = []
1594        out = []
1595        for k in lists.keys():
1596            if lists[k] is not None and k not in out:
1597                out.append(k)
1598        for k in tmptables.keys():
1599            if tmptables[k] is not None and k not in out:
1600                out.append(k)
1601        for k in verbose:
1602            if k not in out:
1603                out.append(k)
1604        return out
1605
address = 'address'
appointment = 'appointment'
catalog = 'catalog'
category = 'category'
concentration = 'concentration'
cxxkitid = 'cxxkitid'
creationdate = 'creationdate'
derivaldate = 'derivaldate'
email = 'email'
initialamount = 'initialamount'
initialunit = 'initialunit'
first_repositiondate = 'first_repositiondate'
method = 'method'
kitid = 'kitid'
lastlogin = 'lastlogin'
login = 'login'
locationname = 'locationname'
locationpath = 'locationpath'
orga = 'orga'
parentid = 'parentid'
parentoid = 'parentoid'
patientid = 'patientid'
project = 'project'
receiptdate = 'receiptdate'
receptacle = 'receptacle'
repositiondate = 'repositiondate'
restamount = 'restamount'
restunit = 'restunit'
sampleid = 'sampleid'
sampleoid = 'sampleoid'
samplingdate = 'samplingdate'
secondprocessing = 'secondprocessing'
secondprocessingdate = 'secondprocessingdate'
stockprocessing = 'stockprocessing'
stockprocessingdate = 'stockprocessingdate'
trial = 'trial'
type = 'type'
username = 'username'
values = 'values'
xposition = 'xposition'
yposition = 'yposition'
cnftemplate = '\n# settings for traction.\n\n# sampleid sets the idcontainertype code that is used when searching for sampleid.\n# put in a code per db target.\nsampleid: \n <db target>: <an idcontainertype code, e.g. SAMPLEID>\n# patientid sets the idcontainertype code that is used when searching for patientid.\n# put in a code per db target.\npatientid: \n <db target>: <an idcontainertype code, e.g. LIMSPSN>\n\n# idc holds additional idcontainertype codes that will be queryable as command line flags.\nidc:\n - <an idcontainertype code>\n - <another idcontainertype code>\n\n# cxx holds the centraxx version for db target\ncxx:\n <db target>: 3|4\n'
def floatornull(x): 
86def floatornull(x):
87    """
88     floatornull casts to float or returns None. somehow the values passed
89     to Amount need float casts, and float casts don't accept None.
90    """
91    if x is None:
92        return None
93    return float(x)

floatornull casts to float or returns None. somehow the values passed to Amount need float casts, and float casts don't accept None.

def get_ids(idables: list, code: str | None = None) -> list: 
94def get_ids(idables:list, code:str|None=None) -> list:
95    """
96     get_ids returns a list of string ids from a list of Idables (Sample or
97     Patient).
98    """
99    return [ x.id(code) for x in idables ]
